name: Home Office Deduction (Simplified Method)
description: Deduct $5 per square foot of home used for business, up to 300 sq ft ($1,500 max).
conditions: Must use space exclusively and regularly for business. Cannot be an employee.

name: Home Office Deduction (Actual Expenses)
description: Deduct actual expenses proportional to business use percentage of home.
conditions: Must use space exclusively and regularly for business. Requires calculating business use percentage.

name: Internet Service (Business Portion)
description: Deduct the business-use percentage of home internet costs.
conditions: Must allocate based on actual business use percentage. Keep records of usage.

name: Home Utilities (Business Portion)
description: Deduct electricity, gas, water proportional to home office square footage.
conditions: Only available with actual expense method. Based on office-to-home area ratio.

name: Home Insurance (Business Portion)
description: Deduct homeowners or renters insurance proportional to office use.
conditions: Only with actual expense method. Pro-rated by business use percentage.

name: Home Repairs & Maintenance (Business Portion)
description: Deduct repairs and maintenance costs proportional to business use of home.
conditions: General home repairs pro-rated; repairs to office space only are 100% deductible.

name: Home Office Depreciation
description: Depreciate the business-use portion of your home over 39 years.
conditions: Only for homeowners using actual expense method. May trigger depreciation recapture on sale.

name: Home Office Phone Line
description: Deduct a dedicated business phone line or business percentage of shared line.
conditions: Dedicated second line is 100% deductible. Personal line requires business use allocation.

name: Mortgage Interest (Business Portion)
description: Deduct the business-use portion of mortgage interest via home office deduction.
conditions: Only with actual expense method. Remaining personal portion may still be itemized.

name: Property Tax (Business Portion)
description: Deduct the business-use portion of real estate taxes through home office.
conditions: Business portion is a Schedule C deduction; personal portion falls under SALT cap.

name: Security System (Business Portion)
description: Deduct business-use percentage of home security system costs.
conditions: Pro-rated by business use percentage. Include monitoring fees and equipment.

name: Standard Mileage Deduction
description: Deduct $0.70 per business mile driven in 2025.
conditions: Must use vehicle for business. Cannot also claim actual vehicle expenses. Keep mileage log.

name: Actual Vehicle Expenses
description: Deduct actual costs of operating a vehicle for business: gas, oil, repairs, insurance, registration.
conditions: Must choose between actual expenses and standard mileage. Track all receipts and business use %.

name: Vehicle Depreciation (MACRS)
description: Depreciate a business vehicle over 5 years using MACRS depreciation tables.
conditions: Only with actual expense method. Luxury vehicle limits apply. Listed property rules.

name: Parking Fees & Tolls
description: Deduct parking fees and tolls for business travel (not commuting).
conditions: Must be for business purposes. Commuting costs are never deductible.

name: Public Transit for Business
description: Deduct costs of public transportation used for business travel.
conditions: Must be for business travel, not regular commuting.

name: Rideshare/Taxi for Business
description: Deduct Uber, Lyft, and taxi fares for business travel.
conditions: Must be for business purposes. Keep receipts and document business reason.

name: Vehicle Lease Payments (Business Portion)
description: Deduct the business-use percentage of vehicle lease payments.
conditions: Inclusion amount may reduce deduction for expensive vehicles. Must use actual expense method.

name: Vehicle Insurance (Business Portion)
description: Deduct the business-use percentage of auto insurance premiums.
conditions: Only with actual expense method. Pro-rated by business mileage percentage.

name: Vehicle Registration & License (Business Portion)
description: Deduct business portion of annual registration and license fees.
conditions: Only with actual expense method. Based on business use percentage.

name: Heavy SUV/Truck (Over 6,000 lbs GVWR)
description: Vehicles over 6,000 lbs GVWR qualify for higher Section 179 limits up to $30,500 in 2025.
conditions: Vehicle must exceed 6,000 lbs GVWR. Must be used more than 50% for business.

name: Electric Vehicle Credit
description: Tax credit up to $7,500 for qualifying new electric vehicles purchased in 2025.
conditions: Vehicle must meet assembly, battery component, and critical mineral requirements. MAGI limits apply.

name: Laptop/Computer Purchase
description: Deduct the cost of a computer used for business purposes.
conditions: Must be used more than 50% for business. Can expense under Section 179 or depreciate.

name: Monitor/Display Purchase
description: Deduct cost of monitors and displays used for business.
conditions: Must be used primarily for business. Can expense or depreciate.

name: Desk & Office Furniture
description: Deduct cost of desks, chairs, and other office furniture for business use.
conditions: Must be used for business. Items over $2,500 should be capitalized and depreciated or expensed under §179.

name: Section 179 Expensing
description: Immediately expense qualifying business equipment up to $1,250,000 in 2025.
conditions: Phase-out begins at $3,130,000 in total equipment purchases. Must be placed in service during tax year.

name: Bonus Depreciation (60% in 2025)
description: First-year bonus depreciation of 60% for qualifying assets placed in service in 2025.
conditions: Applies to new and used property with recovery period of 20 years or less. Phase-down from 100% continues.

name: Smartphone (Business Use)
description: Deduct cost and monthly plan for a smartphone used for business.
conditions: Deduct only business-use percentage. Keep records of business vs personal use.

name: Server & Networking Equipment
description: Deduct servers, routers, switches, and networking hardware for business.
conditions: Can expense under Section 179 or depreciate over 5 years.

name: Camera & Video Equipment
description: Deduct cameras, lighting, and video equipment used for business content creation.
conditions: Must be used primarily for business. Listed property rules may apply.

name: Printer/Scanner
description: Deduct cost of printers, scanners, and multifunction devices for business.
conditions: Must be used for business. Items under de minimis threshold can be expensed immediately.

name: Tablet/iPad (Business Use)
description: Deduct cost of tablets used for business purposes.
conditions: Must be used more than 50% for business. Track business use percentage.

name: Accounting & Bookkeeping Fees
description: Deduct fees paid to accountants, bookkeepers, and CPA firms.
conditions: Business accounting fees are fully deductible on Schedule C. Personal tax prep fees are not deductible 2018-2025.

name: Legal Fees (Business)
description: Deduct attorney fees for business-related legal matters.
conditions: Must be ordinary and necessary business expense. Personal legal fees are generally not deductible.

name: Consulting Fees
description: Deduct fees paid to business consultants and advisors.
conditions: Must be ordinary and necessary. Issue 1099-NEC if payments exceed $600.

name: Tax Preparation Fees (Business)
description: Deduct costs of preparing business tax returns (Schedule C, partnership, S-corp).
conditions: Only business portion of tax prep is deductible. Personal return prep is not deductible through 2025.

name: Financial Planning Fees (Business)
description: Deduct fees for business financial planning and advisory services.
conditions: Must be directly related to business operations. Personal financial planning is not deductible.

name: Payroll Service Fees
description: Deduct costs of payroll processing services like ADP, Gusto, or QuickBooks Payroll.
conditions: Must have employees or pay yourself as W-2 employee (S-corp).

name: Business Licensing & Permits
description: Deduct fees for required business licenses, permits, and regulatory filings.
conditions: Must be required for business operation. Annual renewal fees are deductible.

name: Entity Formation Costs
description: Deduct up to $5,000 in organizational costs for forming a new business entity.
conditions: First $5,000 deductible in first year if total costs under $50,000. Excess amortized over 180 months.

name: Registered Agent Fees
description: Deduct annual fees paid to a registered agent service for your business entity.
conditions: Must be a required business expense for maintaining entity status.

name: Patent & Trademark Fees
description: Deduct or amortize costs of obtaining patents and trademarks.
conditions: Legal fees can be expensed. Filing fees and costs amortized over 15 years.

name: Continuing Education
description: Deduct costs of courses, seminars, and workshops that maintain or improve job skills.
conditions: Must maintain or improve skills in current trade. Cannot qualify you for a new profession.

name: Professional Certifications
description: Deduct costs of obtaining and maintaining professional certifications.
conditions: Must be related to current profession. Initial licensing for a new career is not deductible.

name: Work-Related Books & Publications
description: Deduct cost of professional books, journals, and trade publications.
conditions: Must be directly related to your business or profession.

name: Student Loan Interest Deduction
description: Deduct up to $2,500 in student loan interest paid during the year.
conditions: Above-the-line deduction. MAGI phase-out: $80,000-$95,000 single, $165,000-$195,000 MFJ.

name: Lifetime Learning Credit
description: Tax credit of 20% of up to $10,000 in qualified education expenses ($2,000 max credit).
conditions: MAGI phase-out: $80,000-$90,000 single, $160,000-$180,000 MFJ. Per return, not per student.

name: American Opportunity Tax Credit
description: Credit up to $2,500 per student for first 4 years of post-secondary education. 40% refundable.
conditions: Student must be enrolled at least half-time. MAGI phase-out: $80,000-$90,000 single, $160,000-$180,000 MFJ.

name: Online Course Subscriptions
description: Deduct subscriptions to business-related online learning platforms.
conditions: Must be related to current trade or business. Keep records linking courses to business.

name: Conference & Seminar Registration
description: Deduct registration fees for business conferences, conventions, and seminars.
conditions: Must be related to your trade or business. Travel to conference may also be deductible.

name: Professional Membership Dues
description: Deduct dues for professional organizations and trade associations.
conditions: Must be related to your profession. Lobbying-related portions are not deductible.

name: Educator Expense Deduction
description: K-12 teachers can deduct up to $300 for unreimbursed classroom supplies.
conditions: Must be K-12 teacher, instructor, counselor, or principal. At least 900 hours of work.

name: Self-Employed Health Insurance Deduction
description: Deduct 100% of health insurance premiums for yourself, spouse, and dependents.
conditions: Cannot exceed net self-employment income. Not eligible if you can participate in employer plan.

name: General Liability Insurance
description: Deduct premiums for business general liability insurance.
conditions: Must be ordinary and necessary business expense.

name: Professional Liability (E&O) Insurance
description: Deduct errors and omissions or malpractice insurance premiums.
conditions: Must be related to your professional practice.

name: Workers Compensation Insurance
description: Deduct workers compensation insurance premiums.
conditions: Required in most states if you have employees.

name: Business Property Insurance
description: Deduct premiums for insuring business equipment, inventory, and property.
conditions: Must insure business property. Home-based business may use business-use percentage.

name: Cyber Liability Insurance
description: Deduct premiums for cyber liability and data breach insurance.
conditions: Must be related to business operations. Increasingly common for businesses handling data.

name: Business Interruption Insurance
description: Deduct premiums for business interruption or business continuity insurance.
conditions: Ordinary and necessary business expense.

name: Key Person Life Insurance
description: Premiums for key person insurance are deductible if the business is not the beneficiary.
conditions: Premiums are NOT deductible if business is the beneficiary. Deductible if part of compensation.

name: Long-Term Care Insurance (Self-Employed)
description: Self-employed can deduct age-based portion of long-term care insurance premiums.
conditions: Deduction limited by age-based caps. Part of self-employed health insurance deduction.

name: Disability Insurance (Business)
description: Deduct business overhead disability insurance premiums.
conditions: Business overhead expense policies are deductible. Personal disability premiums are not.

name: 401(k) Employee Contributions
description: Defer up to $23,500 in employee contributions to a 401(k) plan in 2025.
conditions: Additional $7,500 catch-up for age 50+. New super catch-up of $11,250 for ages 60-63.

name: 401(k) Employer Match/Profit Sharing
description: Employer contributions to 401(k) plan, deductible by the business.
conditions: Total employee + employer contributions cannot exceed $70,000 in 2025 (or $77,500 with catch-up).

name: Solo 401(k) Contributions
description: Self-employed can contribute as both employee ($23,500) and employer (25% of net SE income).
conditions: No employees other than spouse. Total limit $70,000 in 2025 ($77,500 with catch-up).

name: SEP IRA Contributions
description: Contribute up to 25% of net self-employment income, max $70,000 in 2025.
conditions: Must contribute same percentage for all eligible employees. Can be funded until tax filing deadline.

name: SIMPLE IRA Contributions
description: Employee contributions up to $16,500 in 2025, plus employer match or 2% nonelective.
conditions: Catch-up of $3,500 for age 50+. Business must have 100 or fewer employees.

name: Traditional IRA Contribution
description: Contribute up to $7,000 to a traditional IRA ($8,000 if 50+) for potential tax deduction.
conditions: Deductibility depends on participation in employer plan and income. MAGI phase-outs apply.

name: Defined Benefit Plan Contributions
description: Contribute substantial amounts to a defined benefit pension plan, actuarially determined.
conditions: Annual benefit limited to $280,000. Requires actuary. Best for high-income, older self-employed.

name: Retirement Plan Setup Credit
description: Tax credit up to $5,000 per year for 3 years for small employers starting a retirement plan.
conditions: 100 or fewer employees receiving $5,000+ compensation. Covers 100% of startup costs up to $5,000.

name: Saver's Credit (Retirement Savings Contribution Credit)
description: Credit of 10-50% of retirement contributions, up to $1,000 ($2,000 MFJ).
conditions: AGI limits: $38,250 single, $57,375 HOH, $76,500 MFJ. Must be 18+, not a student or dependent.

name: Investment Advisory Fees (Business)
description: Deduct investment management fees related to business investments.
conditions: Must be related to business investment activity. Personal investment fees are not deductible 2018-2025.

name: HSA Contributions
description: Contribute up to $4,300 (self) or $8,550 (family) to a Health Savings Account in 2025.
conditions: Must have qualifying HDHP. Additional $1,000 catch-up for age 55+. Above-the-line deduction.

name: FSA Contributions (Health)
description: Contribute up to $3,300 pre-tax to a health Flexible Spending Account in 2025.
conditions: Use-it-or-lose-it with $640 rollover option. Not available with HSA (limited-purpose FSA excepted).

name: Medical Expenses (Above 7.5% AGI)
description: Deduct unreimbursed medical expenses exceeding 7.5% of AGI.
conditions: Must itemize deductions. Only amount exceeding 7.5% of AGI is deductible.

name: Prescription & Medical Supplies
description: Deduct prescription medications and medical supplies as part of medical expense deduction.
conditions: Only counts toward medical expenses exceeding 7.5% AGI threshold. Must itemize.

name: Dental & Vision Expenses
description: Deduct unreimbursed dental and vision care costs as medical expenses.
conditions: Included in medical expense deduction above 7.5% AGI. Must itemize.

name: Health Insurance Premium Tax Credit
description: Premium tax credit for marketplace health insurance based on household income.
conditions: Purchased through marketplace. Income 100-400% FPL (expanded through 2025). Cannot be offered affordable employer coverage.

name: Medical Travel Expenses
description: Deduct mileage ($0.22/mile in 2025), parking, and tolls for medical travel.
conditions: Part of medical expense deduction above 7.5% AGI. Keep mileage log for medical trips.

name: Long-Term Care Expenses
description: Deduct qualified long-term care services as medical expenses.
conditions: Must be chronically ill individual. Part of medical expense deduction above 7.5% AGI.

name: Dependent Care FSA
description: Contribute up to $5,000 pre-tax for dependent care expenses.
conditions: $5,000 limit for MFJ, $2,500 for MFS. Cannot also claim full dependent care credit on same expenses.

name: COBRA Premium Assistance
description: Employer-paid COBRA premiums may be tax-deductible as a business expense.
conditions: Employer-paid premiums are deductible as compensation expense.

name: Computer Peripherals & Accessories
description: Deduct keyboards, mice, webcams, headsets, and other peripherals for business.
conditions: Must be used for business. Items under $200 typically expensed immediately.

name: Software Subscriptions
description: Deduct SaaS subscriptions, cloud services, and software licenses used for business.
conditions: Must be used for business. Subscription fees are deducted in the year paid.

name: Printer Ink & Paper
description: Deduct printer supplies including ink, toner, and paper for business use.
conditions: Ordinary and necessary business supplies.

name: Stationery & Business Cards
description: Deduct letterhead, envelopes, business cards, and branded stationery.
conditions: Must be used for business purposes.

name: Postage & Shipping
description: Deduct postage, shipping costs, and courier services for business.
conditions: Must be for business-related shipments.

name: Cloud Storage & Backup Services
description: Deduct cloud storage subscriptions (Dropbox, Google Drive, AWS) for business.
conditions: Business-use portion is deductible. Allocate if shared with personal use.

name: Domain Names & Hosting
description: Deduct domain registration, web hosting, and DNS service fees.
conditions: Must be for business websites and services.

name: Project Management & Productivity Tools
description: Deduct subscriptions to tools like Slack, Asana, Notion, Trello for business.
conditions: Must be used for business operations.

name: Accounting Software
description: Deduct QuickBooks, FreshBooks, Xero, and similar accounting software subscriptions.
conditions: Must be used for business bookkeeping and accounting.

name: Office Cleaning Supplies
description: Deduct cleaning supplies and janitorial products for business premises.
conditions: Must be for business workspace. Home office requires business-use allocation.

name: Website Design & Development
description: Deduct costs of building or redesigning a business website.
conditions: Ongoing maintenance is expensed. Initial build costs may need to be amortized.

name: Social Media Advertising
description: Deduct Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, and other social media ad spending.
conditions: Must be for business promotion. Keep records of campaigns and business purpose.

name: Google Ads & PPC Advertising
description: Deduct pay-per-click advertising costs on Google, Bing, and other search engines.
conditions: Must be for business advertising purposes.

name: Print Advertising
description: Deduct newspaper, magazine, flyer, and brochure advertising costs.
conditions: Must be business advertising. Political advertising is not deductible.

name: Promotional Materials & Swag
description: Deduct branded promotional items, giveaways, and marketing materials.
conditions: Must have business name/logo. Items over $4 must be in category of signs, display racks, or promotional materials.

name: SEO & Content Marketing
description: Deduct costs of search engine optimization, content creation, and copywriting services.
conditions: Must be for business promotion and marketing.

name: Email Marketing Services
description: Deduct Mailchimp, ConvertKit, and other email marketing platform subscriptions.
conditions: Must be for business email marketing purposes.

name: Photography & Videography
description: Deduct professional photography and videography for business marketing.
conditions: Must be for business promotional use.

name: Sponsorships & Event Marketing
description: Deduct costs of sponsoring events, teams, or organizations for business exposure.
conditions: Must have legitimate business advertising purpose. Charitable sponsorships may be charitable contributions instead.

name: CRM Software
description: Deduct customer relationship management software subscriptions (Salesforce, HubSpot).
conditions: Must be used for business purposes.

name: Business Airfare
description: Deduct airfare for business travel away from your tax home.
conditions: Must be primarily for business. International trips require allocation if mixed purpose.

name: Hotel & Lodging
description: Deduct hotel and lodging costs while traveling for business.
conditions: Must be away from tax home overnight. Only business days for mixed trips.

name: Rental Car (Business Travel)
description: Deduct rental car costs while traveling for business purposes.
conditions: Must be for business travel. Personal use days must be excluded.

name: Business Meals (50% Deduction)
description: Deduct 50% of meal costs when dining with clients, prospects, or during business travel.
conditions: Must have business discussion. Document who, where, business purpose. Not lavish or extravagant.

name: Per Diem Meals (Travel)
description: Use IRS per diem rates instead of tracking actual meal costs during business travel.
conditions: Must be traveling away from tax home. 50% deduction rate. Rates vary by location.

name: Conference Registration & Fees
description: Deduct registration fees for business conferences during travel.
conditions: Must be related to your business. Conference cannot be a vacation disguised as business.

name: Baggage & Shipping (Business Travel)
description: Deduct baggage fees and costs of shipping business materials during travel.
conditions: Must be incurred during business travel.

name: Travel Wi-Fi & Communication
description: Deduct in-flight Wi-Fi, international phone plans, and communication costs during travel.
conditions: Must be for business communication during business travel.

name: Laundry & Dry Cleaning (Extended Travel)
description: Deduct laundry and dry cleaning costs during extended business travel.
conditions: Must be during business travel away from tax home.

name: Tips (Business Travel)
description: Deduct tips paid during business travel to porters, hotel staff, etc.
conditions: Must be reasonable and incurred during business travel.

name: Employee Meals (Company Events)
description: Deduct 100% of meals at company-wide events, picnics, and holiday parties.
conditions: Must be for all employees, not select group. Company picnics and holiday parties qualify for 100%.

name: Office Rent
description: Deduct rent paid for office space, retail space, or business premises.
conditions: Must be used for business. Rent to related parties must be at fair market value.

name: Co-Working Space Membership
description: Deduct monthly or annual co-working space memberships and day passes.
conditions: Must be used for business purposes.

name: Storage Unit Rental
description: Deduct rental of storage units for business inventory, equipment, or records.
conditions: Must store business-related items.

name: Business Electricity
description: Deduct electricity costs for business premises.
conditions: 100% deductible for dedicated business space. Home office uses business-use percentage.

name: Business Water & Sewer
description: Deduct water and sewer costs for business premises.
conditions: 100% for dedicated business space. Pro-rated for home office.

name: Business Gas/Heating
description: Deduct gas and heating costs for business premises.
conditions: 100% for dedicated business space. Pro-rated for home office.

name: Business Internet Service
description: Deduct internet service for dedicated business premises.
conditions: 100% for business-only location. Home-based must allocate business percentage.

name: Trash Removal (Business)
description: Deduct commercial trash and waste removal for business premises.
conditions: Must be for business premises.

name: Janitorial Services
description: Deduct professional cleaning and janitorial services for business space.
conditions: Must be for business premises.

name: Equipment Leasing
description: Deduct lease payments for business equipment (copiers, machinery, etc.).
conditions: Operating leases are fully deductible. Capital leases treated as purchases.

name: Virtual Office / Mail Service
description: Deduct virtual office, mail forwarding, and business address services.
conditions: Must be used for legitimate business purposes.

name: MACRS Depreciation (5-Year Property)
description: Depreciate computers, vehicles, and office equipment over 5 years using MACRS.
conditions: 200% declining balance method. Half-year or mid-quarter convention applies.

name: MACRS Depreciation (7-Year Property)
description: Depreciate furniture, fixtures, and certain machinery over 7 years using MACRS.
conditions: 200% declining balance method. Includes office furniture and fixtures.

name: MACRS Depreciation (27.5-Year Residential)
description: Depreciate residential rental property over 27.5 years straight-line.
conditions: Building only (not land). Straight-line method required. Mid-month convention.

name: MACRS Depreciation (39-Year Commercial)
description: Depreciate nonresidential commercial property over 39 years straight-line.
conditions: Building only (not land). Straight-line method. Mid-month convention.

name: Section 179 Deduction (General)
description: Elect to expense qualifying business property up to $1,250,000 instead of depreciating.
conditions: Property must be placed in service during tax year. Phase-out at $3,130,000 total purchases.

name: Listed Property Depreciation
description: Special depreciation rules for property that can be used for personal purposes (vehicles, cameras).
conditions: Must be used more than 50% for business. If business use drops below 50%, must recapture.

name: Amortization of Intangibles
description: Amortize goodwill, patents, copyrights, and other intangibles over 15 years.
conditions: Applies to acquired intangible assets. Self-created intangibles have different rules.

name: Startup Cost Amortization
description: Deduct up to $5,000 in startup costs, amortize remainder over 180 months.
conditions: First-year deduction reduced if total startup costs exceed $50,000.

name: Cost Segregation (Accelerated Building Depreciation)
description: Reclassify building components to shorter depreciation lives through a cost segregation study.
conditions: Requires a qualified cost segregation study. Most beneficial for buildings over $1M.

name: Leasehold Improvement Depreciation
description: Depreciate qualified improvement property (QIP) over 15 years with bonus depreciation eligible.
conditions: Interior improvements to nonresidential buildings. Excludes enlargement, elevators, escalators, internal structural framework.

name: De Minimis Safe Harbor Election
description: Expense tangible property items up to $2,500 each ($5,000 with audited financials) instead of capitalizing.
conditions: Per-item limit. Must make annual election on tax return. $5,000 threshold requires applicable financial statement.

name: Cash Charitable Donations
description: Deduct cash donations to qualified 501(c)(3) organizations.
conditions: Limited to 60% of AGI for cash to public charities. Must itemize. Get written acknowledgment for $250+.

name: Property/Non-Cash Donations
description: Deduct fair market value of donated property, clothing, and household items.
conditions: Limited to 30% AGI for appreciated property. Appraisal required for items over $5,000. Form 8283.

name: Charitable Mileage Deduction
description: Deduct $0.14 per mile driven for charitable purposes in 2025.
conditions: Must keep mileage log. Set by statute, not adjusted for inflation.

name: Appreciated Stock Donations
description: Donate appreciated securities to avoid capital gains and deduct full fair market value.
conditions: Must hold stock over 1 year. Limited to 30% of AGI. Eliminates embedded capital gains.

name: Donor-Advised Fund Contribution
description: Contribute to a donor-advised fund for immediate deduction with future grant flexibility.
conditions: Deduction in year of contribution. Cash: 60% AGI limit. Appreciated property: 30% AGI.

name: Qualified Charitable Distribution (QCD)
description: Individuals 70.5+ can distribute up to $105,000 from IRA directly to charity, excluded from income.
conditions: Must be 70.5+. Direct from IRA to charity. Counts toward RMD. Not available from employer plans.

name: Volunteer Expense Reimbursement
description: Deduct unreimbursed out-of-pocket expenses incurred while volunteering for charity.
conditions: Must be directly connected to charitable service. Cannot deduct value of time or services.

name: Conservation Easement Donation
description: Deduct the value of a qualified conservation easement donated to a qualified organization.
conditions: Must be qualified conservation contribution. IRS closely scrutinizes syndicated easement transactions.

name: Charitable Deduction (Business)
description: C-corps can deduct charitable contributions up to 10% of taxable income. Pass-throughs deduct on personal return.
conditions: Pass-through entities report on K-1. Individual deduction requires itemizing.

name: Food Inventory Donations
description: Enhanced deduction for businesses donating food inventory to charitable organizations.
conditions: Deduction is lesser of basis plus half the gain, or twice the basis. Must be wholesome food.

name: State Income Tax Deduction
description: Deduct state and local income taxes paid, subject to SALT cap.
conditions: Combined SALT deduction capped at $10,000 ($5,000 MFS). Must itemize.

name: Property Tax Deduction
description: Deduct real estate property taxes, subject to SALT cap.
conditions: Shares the $10,000 SALT cap with state income tax. Must itemize.

name: SALT Cap Workaround (PTE Tax)
description: Many states allow pass-through entity tax elections to bypass the $10,000 SALT cap.
conditions: Available in 30+ states. Entity pays state tax and deducts on entity return. Members get state credit.

name: State Sales Tax Deduction
description: Deduct state sales tax instead of state income tax if more beneficial.
conditions: Choose sales tax OR income tax (not both). Subject to SALT cap. Use IRS tables or actual receipts.

name: State Tax Credits for Retirement
description: Some states offer credits or deductions for retirement income or contributions.
conditions: State-specific. Check your state tax code. Common in states without income tax exemptions.

name: State R&D Tax Credit
description: Many states offer research and development tax credits in addition to federal.
conditions: Must have qualifying R&D activities. State rules differ from federal. Some states have refundable credits.

name: State Small Business Health Insurance Credit
description: Some states offer additional credits for small businesses providing health insurance.
conditions: State-specific eligibility. Usually for small employers with fewer than 25 employees.

name: State Historic Preservation Credit
description: State-level tax credits for rehabilitating historic buildings.
conditions: Building must be listed or eligible for historic register. Credit percentages vary by state.

name: State Energy Efficiency Credits
description: State-level credits for energy-efficient improvements beyond federal credits.
conditions: State-specific. May cover solar, EV charging, insulation, windows, and HVAC.

name: State Opportunity Zone Benefits
description: Some states offer additional tax benefits for investments in designated opportunity zones.
conditions: Must invest in a Qualified Opportunity Fund in a designated zone. State rules may differ from federal.

name: Mortgage Interest Deduction
description: Deduct interest on mortgage debt up to $750,000 for primary and secondary residences.
conditions: Acquisition debt up to $750,000 ($375,000 MFS). Pre-12/15/2017 debt grandfathered at $1M. Must itemize.

name: Student Loan Interest Deduction
description: Deduct up to $2,500 of student loan interest paid as an above-the-line deduction.
conditions: MAGI phase-out: $80,000-$95,000 single, $165,000-$195,000 MFJ. Cannot be claimed as dependent.

name: Business Loan Interest
description: Deduct interest on loans used for business purposes.
conditions: Loan proceeds must be used for business. Business interest limitation may apply for large businesses.

name: Business Credit Card Interest
description: Deduct interest on credit card charges that are business expenses.
conditions: Must be business charges. Personal credit card interest is never deductible.

name: Home Equity Loan Interest (Used for Home Improvement)
description: Deduct interest on home equity loans if used to buy, build, or substantially improve your home.
conditions: Combined with first mortgage, total cannot exceed $750,000. Must be used for home improvement.

name: Investment Interest Expense
description: Deduct interest on debt used to purchase taxable investments, up to net investment income.
conditions: Limited to net investment income. Excess carries forward. Form 4952 required.

name: Margin Interest (Investment)
description: Deduct margin interest paid on investment accounts up to net investment income.
conditions: Must itemize. Limited to net investment income. Cannot use against qualified dividends or long-term gains unless elected.

name: Equipment Financing Interest
description: Deduct interest on loans for business equipment purchases.
conditions: Equipment must be used for business. Interest is deductible as a business expense.

name: Real Estate Loan Interest (Investment Property)
description: Deduct mortgage interest on investment/rental real estate properties.
conditions: No $750,000 cap for investment property. Deducted against rental income on Schedule E.

name: Points Paid on Mortgage
description: Deduct points (prepaid interest) paid when obtaining a mortgage on primary residence.
conditions: Purchase mortgage points deductible in year paid. Refinance points amortized over loan life.

name: Child Tax Credit
description: Credit of $2,000 per qualifying child under 17. Up to $1,700 refundable in 2025.
conditions: Child must be under 17, US citizen/resident. MAGI phase-out starts at $200,000 ($400,000 MFJ).

name: Other Dependent Credit
description: Credit of $500 for qualifying dependents who are not qualifying children.
conditions: For dependents who don't qualify for child tax credit (e.g., children 17+, elderly parents).

name: Child and Dependent Care Credit
description: Credit of 20-35% of childcare expenses, up to $3,000 (one child) or $6,000 (two+).
conditions: Child under 13 or disabled dependent. Both spouses must work or be in school. Form 2441.

name: Dependent Care FSA
description: Exclude up to $5,000 in dependent care expenses from taxable income.
conditions: $5,000 MFJ, $2,500 MFS. Reduces eligible expenses for dependent care credit.

name: Adoption Tax Credit
description: Credit up to $17,280 per child for qualified adoption expenses in 2025.
conditions: MAGI phase-out: $252,150-$292,150. Special needs adoption: full credit regardless of expenses.

name: Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC)
description: Refundable credit for low-to-moderate income workers. Max $7,830 with 3+ children in 2025.
conditions: Income limits vary by filing status and children. Investment income must be $11,600 or less.

name: Head of Household Filing Status
description: More favorable tax brackets and higher standard deduction for qualifying single parents.
conditions: Must be unmarried, pay over half household costs, and have qualifying person living with you.

name: Nanny/Au Pair Tax Benefits
description: Household employer can provide dependent care benefits; nanny taxes enable legal deductions.
conditions: Must pay household employment taxes. Enables claiming dependent care credit/FSA.

name: 529 Plan State Tax Deduction
description: Many states offer income tax deductions for 529 education savings plan contributions.
conditions: No federal deduction. 34 states offer deductions or credits. Amounts vary by state.

name: Tuition & Fees for Dependents
description: Education credits (AOTC, LLC) available for qualified tuition and fees paid for dependents.
conditions: Claim AOTC ($2,500 max) or LLC ($2,000 max) for dependent's education. Cannot claim both for same student.

name: Jury Duty Pay Remitted to Employer
description: Deduct jury duty pay you remit to your employer who paid your salary during jury service.
conditions: Above-the-line deduction. Only if employer paid your salary and required you to turn over jury pay.

name: Gambling Losses
description: Deduct gambling losses up to the amount of gambling winnings reported.
conditions: Cannot exceed gambling winnings. Must itemize. Keep detailed records of wins and losses.

name: Moving Expenses (Military Only)
description: Active-duty military can deduct unreimbursed moving expenses for permanent station change.
conditions: Only for active-duty military with permanent change of station orders. Above-the-line deduction.

name: Federal R&D Tax Credit
description: Credit for qualified research expenses. Small businesses can apply against payroll tax.
conditions: Must have qualifying research activities. Startups can offset up to $500,000 in payroll taxes.

name: Work Opportunity Tax Credit (WOTC)
description: Credit for hiring individuals from targeted groups (veterans, ex-felons, etc.).
conditions: Employee must be from targeted group. Max $2,400-$9,600 per employee. Must certify with state agency.

name: Disabled Access Credit
description: Credit of 50% of expenditures between $250-$10,250 for ADA accessibility improvements.
conditions: Small business with revenue under $1M or 30 or fewer employees. Form 8826.

name: Energy Efficient Home Improvement Credit
description: Credit of 30% for qualifying energy improvements: insulation, windows, doors, HVAC, heat pumps.
conditions: Annual limit $3,200. Sub-limits: $1,200 general, $2,000 heat pumps. Must be primary residence.

name: Residential Clean Energy Credit
description: Credit of 30% for solar panels, solar water heaters, geothermal, battery storage, and wind.
conditions: No cap for most items. Must be installed in your home. Battery storage minimum 3 kWh.

name: Qualified Business Income (QBI) Deduction
description: Deduct up to 20% of qualified business income from pass-through entities.
conditions: Phase-out for specified service businesses above $191,950 single / $383,900 MFJ. W-2/property limits may apply.

name: Self-Employment Tax Deduction (50%)
description: Deduct the employer-equivalent portion (50%) of self-employment tax above the line.
conditions: Automatic above-the-line deduction. Calculated on Schedule SE.

name: Bad Debt Deduction
description: Deduct business debts that become worthless during the tax year.
conditions: Must be a bona fide debt. Must be wholly or partially worthless. Document collection efforts.

name: Net Operating Loss (NOL) Carryforward
description: Carry forward net operating losses indefinitely to offset up to 80% of future taxable income.
conditions: 80% of taxable income limit. No carryback (except farming). Carried forward indefinitely.

name: Casualty & Theft Loss (Federally Declared Disaster)
description: Deduct losses from federally declared disasters exceeding 10% of AGI.
conditions: Must be in a federally declared disaster area. Reduced by $100 per event and 10% of AGI.

name: Alimony Payments (Pre-2019 Agreements)
description: Deduct alimony paid under divorce agreements executed before January 1, 2019.
conditions: Only for agreements finalized before 1/1/2019 not modified to adopt new rules. Above-the-line.

name: Foreign Tax Credit
description: Credit for income taxes paid to foreign governments on foreign-source income.
conditions: Cannot exceed US tax on foreign-source income. Form 1116. Can choose credit or deduction.

name: Small Employer Health Insurance Credit
description: Credit up to 50% of premiums paid for small businesses providing employee health insurance.
conditions: Fewer than 25 FTE employees. Average annual wages below $58,000. Pay at least 50% of premiums.

name: Employee Retention Credit Carryforward
description: Unused ERC from 2020-2021 may still be claimed via amended returns if eligible.
conditions: Must have been eligible employer during COVID period. IRS moratorium on new claims; consult advisor.

name: Business Vehicle Wash & Detailing
description: Deduct vehicle cleaning and detailing costs for business-use vehicles.
conditions: Only with actual expense method. Pro-rated by business use percentage.

name: Business Meals While Traveling
description: Deduct 50% of meal costs while traveling overnight for business.
conditions: Must be traveling away from tax home. Meals not lavish or extravagant.

name: Employer-Provided Childcare Credit
description: Credit of 25% of qualified childcare facility expenditures plus 10% of resource/referral costs.
conditions: Maximum credit $150,000. Must maintain qualified childcare facility or contract with qualified provider.

name: Business Gifts
description: Deduct business gifts up to $25 per recipient per year.
conditions: Limit is $25 per person per year. Incidental costs like engraving or gift wrapping excluded from limit.

name: Employee Wages & Salaries
description: Deduct reasonable wages, salaries, bonuses, and commissions paid to employees.
conditions: Must be reasonable compensation for services rendered. S-corp owner must take reasonable salary.

name: Employer Payroll Taxes
description: Deduct the employer portion of FICA (Social Security and Medicare) taxes.
conditions: Employer share of Social Security (6.2%) and Medicare (1.45%) on employee wages.

name: Employee Benefits (Non-Insurance)
description: Deduct cost of employee benefit programs: education assistance, transit benefits, wellness programs.
conditions: Must be available to employees. Some benefits have statutory exclusion limits.

name: Bank & Financial Fees (Business)
description: Deduct business bank account fees, merchant processing fees, and wire transfer charges.
conditions: Must be for business accounts. Personal banking fees are not deductible.

name: Contract Labor (1099 Workers)
description: Deduct payments to independent contractors for business services.
conditions: Issue 1099-NEC for payments of $600+. Must be legitimate independent contractor relationship.

name: Uniforms & Work Clothing
description: Deduct cost and maintenance of required uniforms or protective clothing not suitable for everyday wear.
conditions: Must be required for work and not suitable for everyday wear. Safety equipment qualifies.

name: Business Use of Personal Vehicle (Reimbursement)
description: S-corps and employers can reimburse employees for business mileage under an accountable plan.
conditions: Must be under accountable plan with substantiation. Reimbursement at IRS standard rate.

name: Health Reimbursement Arrangement (HRA)
description: Employers can set up HRAs to reimburse employees for medical expenses tax-free.
conditions: QSEHRA limit: $6,350 self-only, $12,800 family in 2025. ICHRA has no cap.

name: Qualified Small Business Stock Exclusion (Section 1202)
description: Exclude up to 100% of gain on qualified small business stock held 5+ years.
conditions: Must hold stock 5+ years. C-corp with gross assets under $50M at issuance. Exclusion up to $10M or 10x basis.

name: Opportunity Zone Investment Deferral
description: Defer and potentially reduce capital gains by investing in Qualified Opportunity Funds.
conditions: Must invest capital gains within 180 days. Hold 10+ years for exclusion of new gains.

name: Mortgage Interest Deduction (Rental Property)
description: Deduct mortgage interest on loans used to acquire or improve rental real estate property.
conditions: Interest must be on debt used to acquire, build, or improve rental property. Report on Schedule E.

name: Property Tax Deduction (Rental)
description: Deduct real estate taxes paid on rental properties as a business expense on Schedule E.
conditions: Rental property taxes are fully deductible as business expense (not subject to SALT cap). Report on Schedule E.

name: Rental Property Depreciation (Residential 27.5-Year)
description: Depreciate residential rental building cost over 27.5 years using straight-line method.
conditions: Building portion only (exclude land). Begins when placed in service. Mid-month convention applies.

name: Commercial Property Depreciation (39-Year)
description: Depreciate nonresidential real property over 39 years using straight-line depreciation.
conditions: Nonresidential real property. 39-year straight-line. Mid-month convention. Excludes land.

name: 1031 Exchange Tax Deferral
description: Defer capital gains tax by exchanging investment real property for like-kind property.
conditions: Must use qualified intermediary. 45-day identification and 180-day closing deadlines. Both properties for investment/business use.

name: Conservation Easement Charitable Deduction
description: Charitable deduction for donating a permanent conservation easement on qualified real property.
conditions: Qualified appraisal required. Easement must be permanent. 50% AGI limit (100% for qualified farmers/ranchers). 15-year carryforward.

name: Rental Repairs and Maintenance
description: Deduct ordinary and necessary repair expenses for rental property in the year incurred.
conditions: Must be repairs (not improvements). Improvements must be capitalized and depreciated. De minimis safe harbor: $2,500 per item.

name: Rental Insurance Premiums
description: Deduct insurance premiums paid on rental properties (fire, liability, flood, etc.).
conditions: Must be ordinary and necessary business insurance for rental activity.

name: Property Management Fees
description: Deduct fees paid to property management companies for managing rental properties.
conditions: Typical fee: 8-12% of rental income. Must be for legitimate management services.

name: Rental Travel Expenses
description: Deduct travel costs to manage, maintain, or collect rent from rental properties.
conditions: Local travel: standard mileage rate or actual expenses. Overnight: reasonable lodging and meals (50% for meals).

name: Depreciation Recapture (Section 1250)
description: Unrecaptured §1250 gain on sale of depreciable real property is taxed at a maximum 25% rate.
conditions: Applies on sale of real property. Recapture equals lesser of gain or accumulated depreciation. Maximum 25% tax rate.

name: Historic Rehabilitation Tax Credit
description: 20% federal tax credit for qualified rehabilitation expenses on certified historic structures.
conditions: Certified historic structure. Substantial rehabilitation test. Credit claimed ratably over 5 years. Must reduce depreciable basis.

name: Low-Income Housing Tax Credit (LIHTC)
description: Federal tax credit for investment in qualified low-income housing projects, claimed over 10 years.
conditions: Allocated by state housing agencies. 15-year compliance period. Rent and income restrictions on tenants.

name: Installment Sale Interest Income Deferral
description: Spread gain recognition from property sales over the installment period, deferring tax liability.
conditions: At least one payment after tax year of sale. Depreciation recapture recognized in year of sale. Interest charges above $5M.

name: Qualified Land Conservation Contribution
description: Enhanced deduction for qualified farmers and ranchers donating conservation easements — up to 100% of AGI.
conditions: Qualified farmer/rancher. Land must remain available for farming. 100% AGI limit. 15-year carryforward.
